text.title: search-everywhere-optimization Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for search-everywhere-optimization as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent recommends "publish more content" without specifying content gaps as article titles and target queries.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vague content volume recommendations are not actionable. Require the agent to output specific gaps as: article title, target query, and format (FAQ, HowTo, comparison).

failure-mode.description: Agent updates publication dates in metadata without updating the actual content.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI engines and Google both detect content freshness via substantive changes, not metadata timestamps. A date update without content change provides no citation benefit.

failure-mode.description: Agent suggests entity relationships (brand associated with specific concepts or people) without verifying what AI engines currently associate with the brand.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Suggested entity relationships may not match the brand's current knowledge graph representation. Incorrect associations can confuse AI engine entity resolution.
